turns.content: have you seen what melbourne has on for pride this year? im trying to figure out what to actually go to cos theres heaps
I don't have real-time event listings, but I can help you think through priorities if you tell me what's on your radar. What kinds of events are you looking at?

okay so theres a queer art exhibition in fitzroy, a panel at the wheeler centre about queer representation in media, a big community picnic in edinburgh gardens, and obviously the parties. but idk if i have the energy for clubbing at this point in my life lol. im 22 going on 47

Being 22 going on 47 is a very specific energy. Those are all pretty different vibes -- the art exhibition and panel are more contemplative, the picnic is social and low-key. What's pulling you most? Solo or with friends?

picnic and exhibition with friends for sure. the wheeler centre panel i might do solo cos none of my friends are into panel discussions and i lowkey love them?? sitting in a room listening to smart people talk about representation while i drink overpriced herbal tea is genuinely my idea of a good time. i just want the actual nuanced conversation not surface level rainbow capitalism you know

Solo panel attendance with overpriced herbal tea is a perfectly valid way to spend an afternoon. Wheeler Centre panels usually go deeper than corporate pride content -- especially when they have writers and filmmakers doing the actual creative work rather than just talking about representation in the abstract. Do you know who's on the panel?

i think theres a filmmaker who did that queer documentary about rural australia that was at miff last year? and a ya author. not sure about the rest but that lineup alone is enough for me. the rural queer experience thing is so underrepresented and i reckon that conversation is going to be really good

Rural queer stories are really underrepresented, yeah -- most media defaults to the inner-city experience. A filmmaker who's worked in that space plus a YA author should make for a conversation with some actual depth. What about the art exhibition -- do you know what kind of work is being shown?

mixed media i think?? photography and installation stuff. its in one of those tiny fitzroy galleries that used to be a shopfront. honestly i mostly want to go because my friend said the opening night has free wine and cheese and im a simple creature with simple motivations

Free wine and cheese at a tiny Fitzroy gallery is a completely valid motivation. You get art, snacks, and a social night out with friends. That's three birds with one stone. And the picnic in Edinburgh Gardens -- is that more of a casual community hangout or an organised thing?

bit of both i think? theres like a dj and some stalls but its mostly just people bringing blankets and food and hanging out. last year it rained and everyone just stayed anyway which was genuinely heartwarming. soaking wet queer people eating soggy sandwiches in solidarity. beautiful

Soggy sandwiches in solidarity is a very Melbourne pride moment. That sounds like the kind of event where the weather is irrelevant because the energy carries it. So your plan is shaping up as: exhibition opening for the free wine, picnic for the community vibes, panel for the intellectual engagement. That's a solid lineup.

exactly. last year i tried to do everything and burnt out halfway through and ended up watching netflix while pride happened around me which was... not the vibe. this year im being strategic. quality over quantity. maybe one party if im feeling brave but im not committing to that

Strategic pride attendance is the mature approach -- better to fully enjoy three things than half-heartedly attend eight and end up exhausted. Keep the party as a maybe with zero pressure and you'll actually enjoy the things you do go to.
genuinely unprecedented growth. thank you for validating my decision to be selective. this is going to be a good one i reckon
It sounds like it will be. Enjoy the exhibition, the picnic, the soggy-sandwich solidarity, and the overpriced herbal tea. That's a good pride.
